content:
# How to Supercharge Intercom Fin With Live Intelligence

Intercom Fin is good at one thing: reading your help center and giving customers answers based on what you have already written down.

That is also its biggest limitation.

Fin does not know what your competitors just launched. It does not know the funding round your prospect just closed. It does not know the job listing your target company just posted. It only knows what you fed it last Tuesday.

If your Fin is answering sales questions, it is doing so from a static knowledge base. And static knowledge has a half-life.

## What Fin Is Missing

Fin works like an open-book exam where the book was written six months ago. The answers are technically correct, but the world has moved on.

Your prospects are not asking about your product in a vacuum. They are asking in context:

- Who else is doing what you do?
- How does your pricing compare to [competitor they just evaluated]?
- What companies like theirs are using you?
- Is this actually going to solve the specific problem they have right now?

A help article cannot answer those questions in real time. A static FAQ cannot adapt to what the prospect just said about their stack, their budget, their timeline.

Fin needs a brain. Not more articles.

## What Forage Adds

Forage is a knowledge layer for AI agents. It gives Fin three things it does not have:

### 1. Live Web Intelligence

Forage pulls live data from the web. Real companies. Real emails. Real funding rounds. Real competitor moves. When a prospect asks about a specific company, Fin can look it up right now — not recall what was written last quarter.

36 tools, one connection. B2B lead generation, competitor analysis, email discovery, company profiling — all available via a single MCP server.

### 2. Memory That Compounds

Every conversation Fin has feeds back into a persistent knowledge graph. 1 million entities deep: companies, people, funding rounds, competitors, technologies, buying signals.

When Fin talks to prospect A about the FinTech vertical, it learns. When prospect B comes in from the same vertical an hour later, Fin already knows the landscape. It does not start from zero.

Your help center is static. Forage is alive.

### 3. Causal Reasoning, Not Retrieval

Fin retrieves. Forage reasons.

Ask what is upstream of a company failure. Simulate a market shock downstream. Trace contagion across competitors, supply chains, and hiring signals. Fin can now answer the why questions, not just the what questions.

This is the difference between a chatbot that reads your FAQ and an advisor that actually understands your market.

## How It Works

Forage runs as an MCP server. Connect it to your Intercom Fin setup with one Apify token.

1. Fin receives a question it cannot answer from help articles alone
2. Forage pulls live data from the relevant sources
3. The knowledge graph provides context from prior conversations
4. Fin responds with current, contextual, sourced intelligence
5. The conversation feeds back into the graph for future sessions

No extra dashboards. No manual updates. The intelligence layer compounds.

## The Before and After

**Before (Fin with help articles only):**

> Prospect: Who are your main competitors?
>
> Fin: Forage competes with various AI agent platforms. Our key differentiators include our knowledge graph and pay-per-call pricing.

Generic. Could be any competitor's website.

**After (Fin powered by Forage):**

> Prospect: Who are your main competitors?
>
> Fin: The main alternatives are [Company A] which focuses on RAG-based retrieval without a graph layer, [Company B] which offers agent memory but without live data feeds, and [Company C] which does web scraping but no causal reasoning. The gap most teams hit with those tools is that their agents still reset each session. What specifically are you comparing against — memory, live data, or reasoning?

Specific. Contextual. Turns the answer into a qualification question.

## Pricing

Forage is pay per call. $5 free credits on signup, no credit card required. Most users spend $15 to 60 dollars per month.

That is less than most teams spend on a single SaaS tool. And it turns your Fin from a glorified search bar into an actual intelligence asset.

## Try It

If your Fin is answering sales questions from a help center written in January, it is already behind.

[Start with $5 free credits](https://useforage.xyz/#signup) — one Apify token, 36 tools, and a knowledge graph that never forgets.

If you are already using Intercom and want to see what Fin looks like with live intelligence behind it, [sign up for free](https://app.intercom.com/admins/referral?referral_code=f4981688c801) and connect Forage in under 30 minutes.

faqSchema.question: How does Forage integrate with Intercom Fin?
faqSchema.answer: Forage runs as an MCP server. Connect it to your Fin setup using one Apify token. Fin can then call Forage's 36 tools to pull live web data, query the knowledge graph, and return contextual answers instead of static help article responses.

faqSchema.question: What is the knowledge graph and why does Fin need it?
faqSchema.answer: The Forage knowledge graph contains approximately 1 million entities: companies, people, funding rounds, competitors, technologies, and buying signals. Every conversation feeds back into it. When Fin answers a question, it draws from both live data and everything it has learned from prior sessions, so intelligence compounds over time.

faqSchema.question: Does Forage replace Intercom's help center?
faqSchema.answer: No. Forage augments Fin by adding live intelligence and memory. Your help center remains the foundation for common questions. Forage handles the dynamic questions that require current data, competitive context, or reasoning across entities.

faqSchema.question: How much does Forage cost?
faqSchema.answer: Forage is pay per call with no subscription. You get $5 free credits on signup with no credit card required. Most users spend $15 to $60 per month depending on usage volume.

faqSchema.question: What clients does Forage work with?
faqSchema.answer: Forage works with Claude Desktop, n8n, GPT-4, Apify, and any MCP client. It connects via a single Apify token and gives you access to 36 tools including B2B lead generation, competitor intelligence, company profiling, and more.
